target.slice: README build and test tooling boundary
target.writeBoundary: local doc-only README change; do not modify cJSON source, headers, tests, CMake, Makefile, public API, parser behavior, or printer behavior

userConfirmedIntent.summary: Use cJSON as the first open-source retrofit dogfood target and apply only a local README build/test documentation clarification.
includedBehavior: Recover high-level project intent from README, CMakeLists, Makefile, source, and tests.
Create a graph-source and instruction pack candidate.
Apply only a local README clarification about CMake full test coverage versus the limited Makefile test path.
excludedBehavior: Do not change cJSON parser or printer behavior.
Do not change public headers or ABI/API.
Do not change CMake or Makefile behavior.
Do not create an upstream PR.

observedAnchors.anchor: Ultralightweight JSON parser in ANSI C
observedAnchors.meaning: The product identity emphasizes a tiny ANSI C JSON parser.

observedAnchors.anchor: Because the entire library is only one C file and one header file
observedAnchors.meaning: The simplest consumption model is copyable source/header usage.
CMake is an official build path for the C library.
CMake test construction is a first-class verification surface.
The Makefile keeps the ANSI C/C89 compatibility intent visible.

observedAnchors.anchor: unity_tests parse_examples parse_number parse_string parse_array parse_object print_string print_number
observedAnchors.meaning: Unit tests cover parser and printer behavior through many small executables.

candidateMatch.recommendedShape: Apply a doc-only README clarification around build/test tooling expectations without changing code or build behavior.
candidateMatch.whyThisShape: The local environment lacks cmake/gcc/cl, so behavior-level dogfood would be unverifiable here; README/build boundary documentation is the smallest safe open-source candidate.
risksIfWrong: Editing parser/printer code without maintainer intent could break core behavior.
Editing build files without local toolchain verification could introduce untested build drift.
Treating README-observed intent as maintainer approval would violate retrofit truth boundaries.

implementationPlan.expectedFlow: Add only a README clarification that parser/printer/public API behavior changes should prefer the CMake test suite, while the Makefile test path remains a limited simple check.
nonGoals: No behavior or public API change.

forbiddenFlows.reason: The first open-source dogfood candidate must not change core library behavior.
No local C toolchain is available to verify build-system changes.
No behavior change is planned; tests should not be edited to mask uncertainty.

build.summary: cmake, gcc, and cl were unavailable in this Windows environment; no C build or unit test was run.

runtime.summary: No runtime behavior is changed in this planned read-only/doc-only candidate.

hardware.summary: cJSON has no hardware validation surface.

remainingRisks: No local C build/test toolchain is available.
The local README change has not been proposed upstream.
Any future behavior edit requires a new graph-backed record and build/test toolchain.
nextAllowedActions: Install or use a C toolchain before any behavior-level cJSON change.
Do not modify source, headers, build files, tests, or public API in this first dogfood.
